Therefore, his (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) decision is a decision from him by his authority, and it is also a decision from Allah, the Glorified, because He is the One who established his authority and makes his command effective. The context of His saying: إِذا قَضَى اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَمْراً (when Allah and His Apostle have decided on a matter) testifies to this, as He made a single "matter" (أمر) the object of the decision of both Allah and the Messenger together; this indicates that the intended meaning of "decision" is the disposal of the people's affairs, rather than the legislative enactment (الجعل التشريعي) which is exclusive to Allah.

His saying: وَما كانَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ وَلا مُؤْمِنَةٍ (A faithful man or woman may not) means it is neither proper nor right for any of the believers, male or female, to establish for themselves a choice (الاختيار) in their affairs such that they choose whatever they wish. His saying: إِذا قَضَى اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَمْراً (when Allah and His Apostle have decided on a matter) serves as the temporal condition for the negation of this choice.

The two plural pronouns in His saying: لَهُمُ الْخِيَرَةُ مِنْ أَمْرِهِمْ (have any option in their decision) refer to "the faithful man" and "the faithful woman," which here denote the entire body of believers because they occur in the context of a negation. The explicit noun was replaced by a pronoun in the phrase مِنْ أَمْرِهِمْ (in their decision) rather than saying "have any option in it" to indicate the source of the illusion of having a choice, which is the attribution of the matter to themselves.

The meaning is: It is not for any believer, male or female, when Allah and His Messenger have decided to dispose of one of their affairs, to establish for themselves a choice regarding it on the grounds of its attribution to them and its being one of their affairs, thereby choosing something other than what Allah and His Messenger have decided. Rather, they must follow the will of Allah and His Messenger.

The verse is general, but because it occurs in the context of the subsequent verses, it may serve as a preparation for what is to come in His saying: ما كانَ مُحَمَّدٌ أَبا أَحَدٍ مِنْ رِجالِكُمْ — Muhammad is not the father of any of your men (33:40). It appears from this that some had objected to the Prophet’s (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) marriage to the wife of Zayd and reproached him on the grounds that she was the wife of his son—meaning his adopted son. Some details related to this will follow in the discussion of narrations.

His saying, the Exalted: وَإِذْ تَقُولُ لِلَّذِي أَنْعَمَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَأَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ وَاتَّقِ اللهَ — When you said to him whom Allah had blessed and whom you [too] had blessed, ‘Retain your wife for yourself, and be wary of Allah’ (33:37) until the end of the verse. The one whom Allah had blessed and the Prophet had blessed is Zayd ibn Harithah, who was a slave to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), then he manumitted him and took him as a son. Under him was Zaynab bint Jahsh, the daughter of the Prophet’s (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) paternal aunt. Zayd came to the Prophet to consult him regarding divorcing Zaynab, and the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) forbade him from divorce. Later, Zayd divorced her, and the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) married her, whereupon these verses were revealed.

So, His saying: أَنْعَمَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ (whom Allah had blessed) means through guidance (الهداية) to faith and by making him beloved to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). His saying: وَأَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِ (and whom you [too] had blessed) means through your kindness to him, manumitting him, and singling him out for yourself. His saying: أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ وَاتَّقِ اللهَ (Retain your wife for yourself, and be wary of Allah) is an allusion to refraining from divorcing her; it is not devoid of an indication of Zayd’s insistence on divorcing her.

His saying: وَتُخْفِي فِي نَفْسِكَ مَا اللهُ مُبْدِيهِ (and you were hiding in your heart what Allah was to expose) means what He was to make manifest. وَتَخْشَى النَّاسَ وَاللهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَخْشاهُ (and you feared the people, though Allah is worthier that you should fear Him). The conclusion of the verses, namely His saying: الَّذِينَ يُبَلِّغُونَ رِسالاتِ اللهِ وَيَخْشَوْنَهُ وَلا يَخْشَوْنَ أَحَداً إِلَّا اللهَ — such as deliver the messages of Allah and fear Him, and fear no one except Allah (33:39), is evidence that his (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) fear of the people was not a fear for himself. Rather, it was a fear for the sake of Allah. He hid in his soul what he hid because of a sense that if he were to manifest it, the people would find fault with him and some of those in whose hearts is a sickness would slander him, which would have a negative impact on the faith of the common folk. This fear—as you can see—is not a blameworthy fear, but a fear for the sake of Allah, which is in reality a fear of Allah, the Glorified.

His saying: وَتَخْشَى النَّاسَ وَاللهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَخْشاهُ (and you feared the people, though Allah is worthier that you should fear Him) appears to be a form of reproach (العتاب), acting as a deterrent against a certain type of fear of Allah—which is fearing Him through the medium of the people—and a guidance toward another type of fear of Him, the Exalted: that it was more appropriate to fear Allah alone and not the people, and not to hide in his soul what Allah was to expose. This is an excellent witness that Allah had already made it an obligation (فرض) for him to marry the wife of Zayd, whom he had adopted, in order to remove the restriction (الحرج) from the believers regarding marrying the wives of adopted sons. He (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) was hiding this in his soul for a time out of fear of its negative impact on the people, so Allah granted him security from that through His reproach, similar to what was mentioned previously in His saying: يا أَيُّهَا الرَّسُولُ بَلِّغْ ما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ — O Apostle! Deliver that which has been sent down to you from your Lord until His saying: وَاللهُ يَعْصِمُكَ مِنَ النَّاسِ — and Allah will safeguard you from the people (5:67).

Thus, the apparent reproach that shines through His saying: وَتَخْشَى النَّاسَ وَاللهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَخْشاهُ is intended for his vindication and the support of his cause against the slanders of the detractors among those in whose hearts is a sickness, similar to what was mentioned previously in His saying: عَفَا اللهُ عنك لِمَ أَذِنْتَ لَهُمْ حَتَّى يَتَبَيَّنَ لَكَ الَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا وَتَعْلَمَ الْكاذِبِينَ — May Allah excuse you! Why did you grant them leave [to stay behind] before those who told the truth were evident to you and you had known the liars? (9:43).

Among the proofs that it is a vindication and support in the form of a reproach is His subsequent saying: فَلَمَّا قَضى زَيْدٌ مِنْها وَطَراً زَوَّجْناكَها — So when Zayd had no further need of her, We wedded her to you (33:37), where He informs of His wedding her to him as if it were a matter outside the Prophet’s (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) will and choice, followed by His saying: وَكانَ أَمْرُ اللهِ مَفْعُولاً — and Allah’s command is bound to be fulfilled (33:37).

So His saying: فَلَمَّا قَضى زَيْدٌ مِنْها وَطَراً زَوَّجْناكَها is a consequence of what preceded in His saying: وَتُخْفِي فِي نَفْسِكَ مَا اللهُ مُبْدِيهِ. "Fulfilling one's need" (قضاء الوطر) for her is an allusion to consummation and enjoyment. His saying: لِكَيْ لا يَكُونَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ حَرَجٌ فِي أَزْواجِ أَدْعِيائِهِمْ إِذا قَضَوْا مِنْهُنَّ وَطَراً — so that there may be no blame on the faithful regarding the wives of their adopted sons when they have no further need of them (33:37) is the explanation for the marriage and the wisdom behind the ruling. His saying: وَكانَ أَمْرُ اللهِ مَفْعُولاً (and Allah’s command is bound to be fulfilled) points to the certainty of its occurrence and emphasizes the ruling.

From this, it becomes clear that what the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) was hiding in his soul was that which Allah had made obligatory (فرض) for him—that he should marry her—and not a personal desire or intense love for her while she was still married, as mentioned by a group of commentators. They excused this by saying it is a natural human state (حالة جبلية) from which humans are hardly safe. This is rejected, firstly, because it denies that one can be so strengthened by divine discipline (التربية الإلهية) as to overcome such things. Secondly, there would be no meaning then for the reproach for concealing it in his soul, for there is no permission in Islam to mention the wives of others or to speak amorously of them.

His saying, the Exalted: ما كانَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ مِنْ حَرَجٍ فِيما فَرَضَ اللهُ لَهُ — There is no blame on the Prophet in respect of what Allah has made obligatory for him (33:38) etc. The term "obligation" (الفرض) means designation and allotment. It is said: "He obligated (فرض) for him such-and-half," meaning He designated it for him and allotted it to him. It is also said that in this context it means permissibility (الإباحة) and authorization. "Restriction" (الحرج) means burden and constriction. The negation of restriction means the negation of its cause, which is the prohibition from what was designated for him.

The meaning is: There was no prohibition upon the Prophet in what Allah designated for him or what Allah permitted for him, such that there would be any restriction upon him in that.

His saying: سُنَّةَ اللهِ فِي الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِنْ قَبْلُ — [such being] Allah’s precedent with those who passed away before (33:38). The word "precedent" (سنة) is a noun placed in the position of a verbal noun (مصدر), thus acting as an absolute object (مفعول مطلق); the implied meaning is: "Allah established that as a precedent (سن الله ذلك سنة)." Those who "passed away before" are the previous prophets and messengers, as evidenced by His subsequent saying: الَّذِينَ يُبَلِّغُونَ رِسالاتِ اللهِ (such as deliver the messages of Allah).

His saying: وَكانَ أَمْرُ اللهِ قَدَراً مَقْدُوراً — and Allah’s command is a decree ordained (33:38) means He decrees from Himself for everyone what suits and fits their condition. The prophets were not prohibited from what Allah had decreed and permitted for others, such that the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) would be prohibited from some of what was decreed and permitted.

His saying, the Exalted: الَّذِينَ يُبَلِّغُونَ رِسالاتِ اللهِ وَيَخْشَوْنَهُ وَلا يَخْشَوْنَ أَحَداً إِلَّا اللهَ — such as deliver the messages of Allah and fear Him, and fear no one except Allah (33:39) etc. The relative pronoun is an exposition of the preceding relative pronoun, namely His saying: الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِنْ قَبْلُ (those who passed away before).

"Godwariness" or "fear" (الخشية) is a specific affection of the heart (تأثر قلبي) resulting from something disliked, and it is sometimes attributed to the cause from which the disliked thing is expected. It is said: "I feared (خشيت) that so-and-so would do such-and-such to me," or "I feared (خشيت) so-and-so lest he do such-and-such to me." The prophets fear Allah and fear no one else because, in their view, there is no effective agent in existence (مؤثر في الوجود) except Allah.

This is different from "fear" (الخوف), which is the expectation of something disliked such that it results in practical avoidance (الاتقاء عملا), whether or not it is accompanied by a cardiac affection. This is a practical matter that is sometimes attributed to the prophets, such as His saying, the Exalted, narrating from Moses (عليه‌السلام): فَفَرَرْتُ مِنْكُمْ لَمَّا خِفْتُكُمْ — So I fled from you when I was afraid of you (26:21), and His saying regarding the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله): وَإِمَّا تَخافَنَّ مِنْ قَوْمٍ خِيانَةً — If you fear treachery from any people (8:58). This is the primary meaning of "fear" (الخوف) and "godwariness" (الخشية), though they are sometimes used as synonyms.

From what has preceded, it becomes clear that "godwariness" (الخشية) is negated for the prophets (عليهم‌السلام) absolutely, even though the context of His saying: يُبَلِّغُونَ رِسالاتِ اللهِ وَيَخْشَوْنَهُ (deliver the messages of Allah and fear Him) suggests that what is negated is fear in the delivery of the message. However, all the actions of the prophets, like their words, are part of the delivery (التبليغ); thus, fearlessness in the matter of delivery encompasses all their actions.

His saying: وَكَفى بِاللهِ حَسِيباً — and Allah suffices as a reckoner (33:39) means as an accountant who takes account of the small and the great; therefore, He must be feared, and none other than Him should be feared.

His saying, the Exalted: ما كانَ مُحَمَّدٌ أَبا أَحَدٍ مِنْ رِجالِكُمْ وَلكِنْ رَسُولَ اللهِ وَخاتَمَ النَّبِيِّينَ — Muhammad is not the father of any of your men, but he is the Apostle of Allah and the Seal of the Prophets (33:40) etc. There is no doubt that the verse is intended to refute their objection to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) that he married the wife of his son. The gist of the refutation is that he is not the father of Zayd, nor the father of any of the men present at the time of the address, such that his marriage to the wife of one of them after him would be a marriage to the wife of his son. The address in His saying: مِنْ رِجالِكُمْ (of your men) is to the people present at the time of the verse's revelation. What is meant by "men" (الرجال) is the opposite of women and children. The negation of fatherhood is a generative negation (نفي تكويني), not a legislative one, and the sentence does not contain any legislation.

The meaning is: Muhammad (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) is not the father of any of these men who are your men, such that his marriage to the wife of one of them after him would be a marriage to the wife of his son. Zayd is one of these men, so marrying [his wife] after his divorce is not truly a marriage to a son's wife. As for his adoption of Zayd, no effects of fatherhood or sonship result from it: "He has not made your adopted sons your sons."

As for al-Qasim, al-Tayyib, al-Tahir, and Ibrahim, they were his sons in reality, but they died before reaching maturity; thus, they were not "men" (رجال) such that they would contradict the verse. Likewise, al-Hasan and al-Husayn are the sons of the Messenger of Allah, but the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) passed away before they reached the stage of manhood.

From what has preceded, it is clear that the verse does not necessitate the negation of his (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) fatherhood to al-Qasim, al-Tayyib, al-Tahir, and Ibrahim, nor to the two Hasans, because you have seen that it is specific to the men present at the time of revelation in the state of manhood.

His saying: وَلكِنْ رَسُولَ اللهِ وَخاتَمَ النَّبِيِّينَ (but he is the Apostle of Allah and the Seal of the Prophets). The "Seal" (الخاتم)—with a fathah on the ta—is that with which something is sealed, like a stamp or a mold, meaning that with which an impression is made or a shape is cast. The meaning of his being the "Seal of the Prophets" is that prophethood (النبوة) was concluded with him (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), so there is no prophet after him.

You have already learned the meaning of "messengership" (الرسالة) and "prophethood" (النبوة), and that the Messenger (الرسول) is the one who carries a message from Allah to the people, while the Prophet (النبي) is the one who carries the news of the Unseen (الغيب)—which is the religion and its realities. It follows from this that messengership is removed with the removal of prophethood, for messengership is among the news of the Unseen. If these news are cut off, messengership is cut off.

From here, it becomes clear that his (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) being the Seal of the Prophets necessitates his being the Seal of the Messengers.

In the verse, there is an allusion that his (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) connection and relation to you is the relation of messengership and prophethood, and that what he did was by the command of Allah, the Glorified.

His saying: وَكانَ اللهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيماً (and Allah has knowledge of all things) means that what He explained to you was only by His knowledge.

(Discussion of Narrations)

In al-Durr al-Manthur, Ibn Jarir narrated from Ibn Abbas who said: "The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) proposed to Zaynab bint Jahsh on behalf of Zayd ibn Harithah. She disdained him and said: 'I am better than him in lineage'—and she was a woman with a sharp temperament. Then Allah revealed: وَما كانَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ وَلا مُؤْمِنَةٍ (A faithful man or woman may not...) the entire verse."

I say: There are other narrations with the same meaning.

In the same source, Ibn Abi Hatim narrated from Ibn Zayd who said: "It was revealed regarding Umm Kulthum bint Uqbah ibn Abi Mu’ayt—she was the first woman to migrate—and she offered herself to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). He married her to Zayd ibn Harithah, so she and her brother were displeased. She said: 'We only wanted the Messenger of Allah, but he married us to his slave.' Then it was revealed."

I say: These two narrations are more like an application (تطبيق) of the verse than the actual occasion of revelation (سبب النزول).

In al-Uyun, in the chapter on the assembly of Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) with al-Ma’mun—along with the followers of various religions—in a tradition where he answers the question of Ali ibn al-Jahm regarding the infallibility (عصمة) of the prophets:

He said: "As for Muhammad (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَتُخْفِي فِي نَفْسِكَ مَا اللهُ مُبْدِيهِ ـ وَتَخْشَى النَّاسَ وَاللهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَخْشاهُ (and you were hiding in your heart what Allah was to expose, and you feared the people, though Allah is worthier that you should fear Him), indeed Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, informed His Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) of the names of his wives in the worldly abode and the names of his wives in the Hereafter, and that they are the Mothers of the Believers. One of those named for him was Zaynab bint Jahsh—and she was at that time under Zayd ibn Harithah. So he (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) hid her name in his soul and did not expose it, lest any of the hypocrites say: 'He said regarding a woman in a man's house that she is one of his wives among the Mothers of the Believers,' and he feared the talk of the hypocrites.

Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَتَخْشَى النَّاسَ وَاللهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَخْشاهُ (and you feared the people, though Allah is worthier that you should fear Him), meaning in your soul..." the tradition continues.

I say: Something close to this was narrated in the same source from him (peace be upon him) in response to al-Ma’mun’s question to him regarding the infallibility of the prophets.

In al-Majma’, regarding His saying, the Exalted: وَتُخْفِي فِي نَفْسِكَ مَا اللهُ مُبْدِيهِ, it was said: "That which he hid in his soul was that Allah, the Glorified, had informed him that she would be among his wives and that Zayd would divorce her. When Zayd came and said to him: 'I want to divorce Zaynab,' he said to him: 'Retain your wife for yourself.' Then the Glorified said: 'Why did you say: "Retain your wife for yourself," when I had informed you that she would be among your wives?'" This was narrated from Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon him).

In al-Durr al-Manthur, Ahmad, Abd ibn Humayd, al-Bukhari, al-Tirmidhi, Ibn al-Mundhir, al-Hakim, Ibn Mardawayh, and al-Bayhaqi in his Sunan narrated from Anas who said: "Zayd ibn Harithah came complaining about Zaynab to the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), so the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) began saying: 'Be wary of Allah and retain your wife for yourself.' Then there was revealed: وَتُخْفِي فِي نَفْسِكَ مَا اللهُ مُبْدِيهِ (and you were hiding in your heart what Allah was to expose)."

Anas said: "If the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) were to have concealed anything, he would have concealed this verse." Then the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) married her... the tradition continues.

I say: The narrations in this regard are numerous, although many of them are not free from issues. In the narrations: "The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) did not provide a wedding feast (أولم) for any of his wives as he did for Zaynab; he slaughtered a sheep and fed the people bread and meat." In the narrations, she used to boast over the other wives of the Prophet regarding three things: that her grandfather and the Prophet’s (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) grandfather were one—for she was the daughter of Umaymah bint Abd al-Muttalib, the Prophet’s (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) paternal aunt; that the one who married her to him was Allah, the Glorified; and that the intermediary was Gabriel.

In al-Majma’, regarding His saying, the Exalted: وَلكِنْ رَسُولَ اللهِ وَخاتَمَ النَّبِيِّينَ (but he is the Apostle of Allah and the Seal of the Prophets), a sound tradition is narrated from Jabir ibn Abdillah from the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) who said: إنما مثلي في الأنبياء كمثل رجل بنى دارا ـ فأكملها وحسنها إلا موضع لبنة ، فكان من دخلها ونظر إليها فقال : ما أحسنها إلا موضع هذه اللبنة. قال صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله : فأنا موضع اللبنة ختم بي الأنبياء — "My likeness among the prophets is but like a man who built a house, completed it, and beautified it except for the place of a single brick. Whoever entered it and looked at it would say: 'How beautiful it is, except for the place of this brick!' I am the place of that brick; the prophets were concluded with me." al-Bukhari and Muslim recorded it in their Sahihs.

I say: Others besides them, such as al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa’i, Ahmad, and Ibn Mardawayh, narrated this meaning from others besides Jabir, such as Abu Sa’id and Abu Hurayrah.

In al-Durr al-Manthur, Ibn al-Anbari narrated in al-Masahif from Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Sulami who said: "I was teaching al-Hasan and al-Husayn to recite, and Ali ibn Abi Talib passed by me while I was teaching them. He said to me: 'Teach them to recite خاتم النبيين (Seal of the Prophets) with a fathah on the ta.'"

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ذِكْرًا كَثِيرًا (٤١)
O you who have faith! Remember Allah with frequent remembrance, (41)
وَسَبِّحُوهُ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلًا (٤٢)
and glorify Him morning and evening. (42)
هُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُصَلِّى عَلَيْكُمْ وَمَلَـٰٓئِكَتُهُۥ لِيُخْرِجَكُم مِّنَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ إِلَى ٱلنُّورِ ۚ وَكَانَ بِٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَحِيمًا (٤٣)
It is He who blesses you—and so do His angels—that He may bring you out from darkness into light, and He is most merciful to the faithful. (43)
تَحِيَّتُهُمْ يَوْمَ يَلْقَوْنَهُۥ سَلَـٰمٌ ۚ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ أَجْرًا كَرِيمًا (٤٤)
The day they encounter Him, their greeting will be, ‘Peace,’ and He holds in store for them a noble reward. (44)
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ إِنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ شَـٰهِدًا وَمُبَشِّرًا وَنَذِيرًا (٤٥)
O Prophet! Indeed We have sent you as a witness, as a bearer of good news and as a warner (45)
وَدَاعِيًا إِلَى ٱللَّهِ بِإِذْنِهِۦ وَسِرَاجًا مُّنِيرًا (٤٦)
and as a summoner to Allah by His permission, and as a radiant lamp. (46)
وَبَشِّرِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ بِأَنَّ لَهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ فَضْلًا كَبِيرًا (٤٧)
Announce to the faithful the good news that there will be for them a great grace from Allah. (47)
وَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ وَٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ وَدَعْ أَذَىٰهُمْ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ وَكِيلًا (٤٨)
Do not obey the faithless and the hypocrites, and disregard their torments, and put your trust in Allah, and Allah suffices as trustee. (48)

(Commentary)

These are verses that call the believers to remembrance (الذكر) and glorification (التسبيح), giving them glad tidings and promising them a beautiful promise. They address the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) by his noble attributes, commanding him to give glad tidings to the believers and not to obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites. It is possible that these two groups of verses were revealed at different times.

His saying, the Exalted: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا اللهَ ذِكْراً كَثِيراً "O you who have faith! Remember Allah with frequent remembrance" (33:41). Remembrance (الذكر) — the state of consciousness that stands in contrast to forgetfulness — is the directing of perception toward the One remembered. As for the verbal utterance of His names and attributes that point toward Him, it is merely one of the manifestations of remembrance.

His saying, the Exalted: وَسَبِّحُوهُ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلاً "and glorify Him morning and evening" (33:42). Glorification (التسبيح) — the act of declaring Allah free from any deficiency or likeness to creation — is a form of hallowing (التنزيه). Like remembrance, it does not depend solely on verbal utterance, even if the pronouncing of phrases such as "Glory be to Allah" (سبحان الله) is one of its manifestations.

The morning (البكرة) refers to the beginning of the day, and the evening (الأصيل) refers to its end after the afternoon (العصر). The restriction of glorification to the morning and evening is due to the transformation of states that occurs during these times; thus, it is appropriate to glorify and hallow Him from change, transformation, and every occurring deficiency. It is also possible that "morning and evening" together serve as a metaphor for continuity, similar to "night and day" in His saying: يُسَبِّحُونَ لَهُ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ "they glorify Him night and day" (41:38).

His saying, the Exalted: هُوَ الَّذِي يُصَلِّي عَلَيْكُمْ وَمَلائِكَتُهُ لِيُخْرِجَكُمْ مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ إِلَى النُّورِ "It is He who blesses you, and His angels [as well], that He may bring you out from darkness into light" (33:43). The comprehensive meaning of "blessing" or "prayer" (الصلاة), as gleaned from its various usages, is "inclination" (الانعطاف). Thus, its meaning varies according to that to which it is attributed. For this reason, it has been said: blessing from Allah is mercy (الرحمة), from the angels it is seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار), and from people it is supplication (الدعاء). However, that which is attributed to Allah, the Glorified, in the Quran as "blessing" is the mercy specific to the believers—that which results in the felicity of the Hereafter and eternal success. This is why He justified His blessing upon them by saying: لِيُخْرِجَكُمْ مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ إِلَى النُّورِ وَكانَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَحِيماً "that He may bring you out from darkness into light; and He is most merciful to the faithful" (33:43).

In His discourse, the Glorified has linked their forgetting Him to His forgetting them, and their remembering Him to His remembering them. He said: نَسُوا اللهَ فَنَسِيَهُمْ "They forgot Allah, so He forgot them" (9:67), and He said: فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ "Remember Me, and I will remember you" (2:152). His blessing upon them is His remembrance of them through mercy. If they remember Him frequently and glorify Him morning and evening, He blesses them frequently, envelops them in light, and distances them from darkness.

From this, it becomes clear that His saying: هُوَ الَّذِي يُصَلِّي عَلَيْكُمْ "It is He who blesses you..." serves as the justification for His saying: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا اللهَ ذِكْراً كَثِيراً "O you who have faith! Remember Allah with frequent remembrance." The implication of this justification is that if you remember Allah frequently, He will remember you with His mercy frequently and will go to great lengths to bring you out of darkness into light. It can also be inferred that the "darkness" (الظلمات) is the darkness of forgetfulness and heedlessness, while the "light" (النور) is the light of remembrance.

In His saying: وَكانَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَحِيماً "and He is most merciful to the faithful," the explicit noun "the faithful" (المؤمنين) is used in place of a pronoun (i.e., He did not say "and He is most merciful to you") to indicate that the cause of this mercy is the attribute of faith (الإيمان).

His saying, the Exalted: تَحِيَّتُهمْ يَوْمَ يَلْقَوْنَهُ سَلامٌ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ أَجْراً كَرِيماً "Their greeting on the day they encounter Him will be, ‘Peace!’ and He has prepared for them a noble reward" (33:44). The apparent context suggests that "their greeting" (تحيتهم) is an infinitive (مصدر) added to the object; meaning, they will be greeted—in the passive sense—on the day they meet their Lord, by their Lord and His angels, with "Peace" (السلام). This means that on the day of the encounter, they will be in security and peace, where no harm shall befall them and no punishment shall touch them.

His saying: وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ أَجْراً كَرِيماً "and He has prepared for them a noble reward" means that Allah has readied for them a magnificent recompense.

His saying, the Exalted: يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِنَّا أَرْسَلْناكَ شاهِداً وَمُبَشِّراً وَنَذِيراً "O Prophet! Indeed We have sent you as a witness, as a bearer of good news and as a warner" (33:45). His (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) being a "witness" (شاهداً) over deeds means that he bears witness to them in this worldly realm and will testify to them on the Day of Resurrection. It has been previously explained regarding His saying: لِتَكُونُوا شُهَداءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ وَيَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ عَلَيْكُمْ شَهِيداً "that you may be witnesses to mankind, and that the Apostle may be a witness to you" (2:143) and other verses of witnessing, that he (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) is the witness over all witnesses.

His being a "bearer of good news" (مبشراً) and a "warner" (نذيرا) refers to his conveying glad tidings to the believers who obey Allah and His Messenger regarding Allah's reward and Paradise, and his warning the disbelievers and the disobedient regarding Allah's punishment and the Fire.

His saying, the Exalted: وَداعِياً إِلَى اللهِ بِإِذْنِهِ وَسِراجاً مُنِيراً "and as a summoner to Allah by His permission, and as a radiant lamp" (33:46). His "summoning to Allah" is his calling people to faith in Allah alone, which necessitates faith in the religion of Allah. Restricting the summons to being "by His permission" makes it synonymous with the divine mission (البعثة).

His (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) being a "radiant lamp" (سراجاً منيراً) means that he is the means by which people are guided to their felicity and saved from the darkness of wretchedness and misguidance. This is a metaphor (استعارة). The view of some who claim that the "radiant lamp" refers to the Quran—implying the meaning "possessor of a radiant lamp"—is an unnecessary affectation.

His saying, the Exalted: وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ بِأَنَّ لَهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ فَضْلاً كَبِيراً "And give good news to the faithful that there shall be for them a great bounty from Allah" (33:47). Grace or bounty (الفضل) refers to a bestowal that is given without the recipient having a prior right to it. Allah has described His bestowal by saying: مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ عَشْرُ أَمْثالِها "Whoever brings a good deed shall receive ten times its like" (6:160) and: لَهُمْ ما يَشاؤُنَ فِيها وَلَدَيْنا مَزِيدٌ "There they will have whatever they wish, and with Us there is yet more" (50:35). Thus, He clarified that He gives a reward that far exceeds the deed, and this is "grace." There is no evidence in the verse to suggest that this is restricted to the Hereafter.

His saying, the Exalted: وَلا تُطِعِ الْكافِرِينَ وَالْمُنافِقِينَ وَدَعْ أَذاهُمْ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ "And do not obey the faithless and the hypocrites, and disregard their annoyance, and put your trust in Allah..." (33:48). The meaning of "obeying the disbelievers and the hypocrites" was discussed at the beginning of the Surah.

His saying: وَدَعْ أَذاهُمْ "and disregard their annoyance" means to leave aside the harm they cause you by turning away from it and not becoming preoccupied with it. The evidence for this meaning is His saying: وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ "and put your trust in Allah," meaning do not rely on yourself alone in repelling their harm, but rather make Allah your trustee (وكيلاً) in that matter, and "Allah suffices as a trustee."

(Narrative Discussion)

In al-Kafi, with its chain of transmission from Ibn al-Qaddah, from Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him), who said: ما من شيء إلا وله حد ينتهي إليه إلا الذكر ـ فليس له حد ينتهي إليه ـ فرض الله عز وجل الفرائض ـ فمن أداهن فهو حدهن ـ وشهر رمضان فمن صامه فهو حده ـ والحج فمن حج فهو حده إلا الذكر ـ فإن الله عز وجل لم يرض منه بالقليل ـ ولم يجعل له حدا ينتهي إليه ـ ثم تلا : « يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا اللهَ ذِكْراً كَثِيراً ـ وَسَبِّحُوهُ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلاً » فقال : لم يجعل الله له حدا ينتهي إليه ـ. — "There is nothing except that it has a limit where it ends, except for remembrance (الذكر)—for it has no limit where it ends. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, ordained the obligatory duties—so whoever performs them, that is their limit. And the month of Ramadan—whoever fasts it, that is its limit. And the Hajj—whoever performs the pilgrimage, that is its limit. Except for remembrance, for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is not pleased with a small amount of it, and He did not set for it a limit where it ends." Then he recited: "O you who have faith! Remember Allah with frequent remembrance, and glorify Him morning and evening," and said: "Allah did not set for it a limit where it ends."

He said: وكان أبي كثير الذكر ـ لقد كنت أمشي معه وإنه ليذكر الله وآكل معه الطعام ـ وإنه ليذكر الله ولقد كان يحدث القوم ما يشغله ذلك ـ عن ذكر الله وكنت أرى لسانا لازقا بحنكه يقول : لا إله إلا الله ـ. — "My father [Imam al-Baqir] used to remember Allah frequently. I would walk with him and he would be remembering Allah; I would eat food with him and he would be remembering Allah. He would be speaking to people, yet that would not distract him from the remembrance of Allah. I would see his tongue clinging to his palate, saying: 'There is no god but Allah' (لا إله إلا الله)."

وكان يجمعنا فيأمرنا بالذكر حتى تطلع الشمس ـ ويأمر بالقراءة من كان يقرأ منا ومن كان لا يقرأ منا أمره بالذكر ، والبيت الذي يقرأ فيه القرآن ويذكر الله عز وجل فيه يكثر بركته ويحضره الملائكة ويهجره الشياطين ـ ويضيء لأهل السماء كما يضيء الكوكب لأهل الأرض ـ والبيت الذي لا يقرأ فيه القرآن ـ ولا يذكر الله يقل بركته ويهجره الملائكة ويحضره الشياطين. — "He used to gather us and command us to engage in remembrance until the sun rose. He would command those of us who could recite to recite [the Quran], and those who could not, he would command to engage in remembrance. The house in which the Quran is recited and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is remembered, its blessings increase, the angels attend it, and the devils abandon it; it shines for the inhabitants of heaven just as the stars shine for the inhabitants of earth. But the house in which the Quran is not recited and Allah is not remembered, its blessings decrease, the angels abandon it, and the devils attend it."

And the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: ألا أخبركم بخير أعمالكم ـ أرفعها في درجاتكم وأزكاها عند مليككم ـ وخير لكم من الدينار والدرهم ـ وخير لكم من أن تلقوا عدوكم فتقتلوهم ويقتلوكم؟ فقالوا : بلى. قال : ذكر الله عز وجل كثيرا. — "Shall I not inform you of the best of your deeds—the highest of them in your ranks, the purest of them before your Sovereign, better for you than gold and silver, and better for you than meeting your enemy such that you strike their necks and they strike yours?" They said: "Yes." He said: "Frequent remembrance of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic."

Then he said: جاء رجل إلى النبي صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله فقال : من خير أهل المسجد؟ فقال : أكثرهم لله ذكرا. — "A man came to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and asked: 'Who is the best of the people of the mosque?' He replied: 'The one among them who remembers Allah the most.'"

And the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: من أعطي لسانا ذاكرا ـ فلقد أعطي خير الدنيا والآخرة. — "Whoever is given a remembering tongue has indeed been given the good of this world and the Hereafter."

And he said regarding His saying: وَلا تَمْنُنْ تَسْتَكْثِرُ "and do not confer a favor to seek more" (74:6): لا تستكثر ما عملت من خير لله. — "Do not consider the good you have done for Allah to be much."

In the same source, with its chain of transmission from Abu al-Mu'azza in a marfu‘ narration, he said: Imam ‘Ali (peace be upon him) said: من ذكر الله في السر فقد ذكر الله كثيرا ـ إن المنافقين كانوا يذكرون الله علانية ـ ولا يذكرونه في السر فقال الله عز وجل : « يُراؤُنَ النَّاسَ وَلا يَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ». — "Whoever remembers Allah in secret has indeed remembered Allah frequently. Indeed, the hypocrites used to remember Allah publicly but would not remember Him in secret, so Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'they show off to the people and do not remember Allah except a little' (4:142)."

I say: This is a subtle and profound inference.

In al-Khisal, from Zayd al-Shahham, who said: Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) said: ما ابتلي المؤمن بشيء أشد عليه من ثلاث خصال يحرمها. قيل : وما هي؟ قال : المواساة في ذات يده ، والإنصاف من نفسه ، وذكر الله كثيرا. أما إني لا أقول : سبحان الله والحمد لله ولا إله إلا الله والله أكبر وإن كان منه ـ ولكن ذكر الله عند ما أحل له وذكر الله عند ما حرم عليه. — "A believer is not tested with anything harder for him than three qualities of which he may be deprived." It was asked: "And what are they?" He said: "Consoling others with his wealth, being just toward others against himself, and the frequent remembrance of Allah. Indeed, I do not mean [just] saying: 'Glory be to Allah, praise be to Allah, there is no god but Allah, and Allah is Great'—though that is part of it—but rather remembering Allah regarding what He has made lawful for him and remembering Allah regarding what He has forbidden him."

In al-Durr al-Manthur, Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi, and al-Bayhaqi narrated from Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri: The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) was asked: "Which of the servants is highest in rank before Allah on the Day of Resurrection?" He said: الذاكرون الله كثيرا. — "Those who remember Allah frequently." I said: "O Messenger of Allah, what about the warrior in the way of Allah?" He said: لو ضرب بسيفه في الكفار والمشركين ـ حتى ينكسر ويختضب دما ـ لكان الذاكرون الله أفضل درجة منه. — "Even if he were to strike with his sword among the disbelievers and polytheists until it breaks and he is dyed in blood, those who remember Allah would still be superior to him in rank."

In al-‘Ilal, with its chain of transmission from ‘Abdullah ibn al-Hasan, from his father, from his grandfather al-Hasan ibn ‘Ali (peace be upon him), who said: A group of Jews came to the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), and the most learned among them asked him several questions, among which he asked: "Why were you named Muhammad, Ahmad, Abu al-Qasim, Bashir (the bearer of glad tidings), Nadhir (the warner), and Da‘i (the summoner)?" He (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) replied: أما الداعي فإني أدعو الناس إلى دين ربي عز وجل ، وأما النذير فإني أنذر بالنار من عصاني ، وأما البشير فإني أبشر بالجنة من أطاعني. — "As for the Summoner, I summon people to the religion of my Lord, the Mighty and Majestic. As for the Warner, I warn with the Fire whoever disobeys me. As for the Bearer of Glad Tidings, I give glad tidings of Paradise to whoever obeys me." (The tradition continues).

In the Tafsir of al-Qummi, regarding His saying: يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِنَّا أَرْسَلْناكَ — up to His saying — وَدَعْ أَذاهُمْ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ وَكَفى بِاللهِ وَكِيلاً, it is mentioned that it was revealed in Mecca five years before the Migration (الهجرة).

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا نَكَحْتُمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ ثُمَّ طَلَّقْتُمُوهُنَّ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَمَسُّوهُنَّ فَمَا لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ عِدَّةٍ تَعْتَدُّونَهَا ۖ فَمَتِّعُوهُنَّ وَسَرِّحُوهُنَّ سَرَاحًا جَمِيلًا (٤٩)
O you who have faith! When you marry faithful women and then divorce them before you touch them, there shall be no period for you to reckon. But provide for them and release them in a graceful manner. (49)
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ إِنَّآ أَحْلَلْنَا لَكَ أَزْوَٰجَكَ ٱلَّـٰتِىٓ ءَاتَيْتَ أُجُورَهُنَّ وَمَا مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ مِمَّآ أَفَآءَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ وَبَنَاتِ عَمِّكَ وَبَنَاتِ عَمَّـٰتِكَ وَبَنَاتِ خَالِكَ وَبَنَاتِ خَـٰلَـٰتِكَ ٱلَّـٰتِى هَاجَرْنَ مَعَكَ وَٱمْرَأَةً مُّؤْمِنَةً إِن وَهَبَتْ نَفْسَهَا لِلنَّبِىِّ إِنْ أَرَادَ ٱلنَّبِىُّ أَن يَسْتَنكِحَهَا خَالِصَةً لَّكَ مِن دُونِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۗ قَدْ عَلِمْنَا مَا فَرَضْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ فِىٓ أَزْوَٰجِهِمْ وَمَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُهُمْ لِكَيْلَا يَكُونَ عَلَيْكَ حَرَجٌ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (٥٠)
O Prophet! Indeed We have made lawful to you your wives whom you have given their dowries, and those whom your right hand owns, of those whom Allah gave you as spoils of war, and the daughters of your paternal uncle, and the daughters of your paternal aunts, and the daughters of your maternal uncle, and the daughters of your maternal aunts who migrated with you, and a faithful woman if she offers herself to the Prophet and the Prophet desires to take her in marriage (a privilege exclusively for you, not for [the rest of] the faithful; We know what We have made lawful for them with respect to their wives and those whom their right hands own, so that there may be no blame on you ), and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (50)
۞ تُرْجِى مَن تَشَآءُ مِنْهُنَّ وَتُـْٔوِىٓ إِلَيْكَ مَن تَشَآءُ ۖ وَمَنِ ٱبْتَغَيْتَ مِمَّنْ عَزَلْتَ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ أَدْنَىٰٓ أَن تَقَرَّ أَعْيُنُهُنَّ وَلَا يَحْزَنَّ وَيَرْضَيْنَ بِمَآ ءَاتَيْتَهُنَّ كُلُّهُنَّ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى قُلُوبِكُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَلِيمًا (٥١)
You may put off whichever of them you wish and consort with whichever of them you wish, and there is no sin upon you [in receiving again] any [of them] whom you may seek [to consort with] from among those you have set aside [earlier]. That makes it likelier that they will be comforted and not feel unhappy, and all of them will be pleased with what you give them. Allah knows what is in your hearts, and Allah is all-knowing, all-forbearing. (51)
لَّا يَحِلُّ لَكَ ٱلنِّسَآءُ مِنۢ بَعْدُ وَلَآ أَن تَبَدَّلَ بِهِنَّ مِنْ أَزْوَٰجٍ وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكَ حُسْنُهُنَّ إِلَّا مَا مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ رَّقِيبًا (٥٢)
Beyond that, women are not lawful for you, nor that you should change them for other wives even though their beauty should impress you, except those whom your right hand owns. Allah is watchful over all things. (52)
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَدْخُلُوا۟ بُيُوتَ ٱلنَّبِىِّ إِلَّآ أَن يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ إِلَىٰ طَعَامٍ غَيْرَ نَـٰظِرِينَ إِنَىٰهُ وَلَـٰكِنْ إِذَا دُعِيتُمْ فَٱدْخُلُوا۟ فَإِذَا طَعِمْتُمْ فَٱنتَشِرُوا۟ وَلَا مُسْتَـْٔنِسِينَ لِحَدِيثٍ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكُمْ كَانَ يُؤْذِى ٱلنَّبِىَّ فَيَسْتَحْىِۦ مِنكُمْ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَسْتَحْىِۦ مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ ۚ وَإِذَا سَأَلْتُمُوهُنَّ مَتَـٰعًا فَسْـَٔلُوهُنَّ مِن وَرَآءِ حِجَابٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ أَطْهَرُ لِقُلُوبِكُمْ وَقُلُوبِهِنَّ ۚ وَمَا كَانَ لَكُمْ أَن تُؤْذُوا۟ رَسُولَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَآ أَن تَنكِحُوٓا۟ أَزْوَٰجَهُۥ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦٓ أَبَدًا ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكُمْ كَانَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ عَظِيمًا (٥٣)
O you who have faith! Do not enter the Prophet’s houses for a meal until you are granted permission, without hanging around for it to be readied. But enter when you are invited, and disperse when you have taken your meal, without cozying up for chats. Such conduct on your part offends the Prophet, and he is ashamed of [asking] you [to leave]; but Allah is not ashamed of [expressing] the truth. When you ask [his] womenfolk for something, do so from behind a curtain. That is more chaste for your hearts and theirs. You should not offend the Apostle of Allah, nor may you ever marry his wives after him. Indeed that would be a grave [sin] with Allah. (53)
إِن تُبْدُوا۟ شَيْـًٔا أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمًا (٥٤)
Whether you disclose anything or hide it, Allah indeed knows all things. (54)
لَّا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِنَّ فِىٓ ءَابَآئِهِنَّ وَلَآ أَبْنَآئِهِنَّ وَلَآ إِخْوَٰنِهِنَّ وَلَآ أَبْنَآءِ إِخْوَٰنِهِنَّ وَلَآ أَبْنَآءِ أَخَوَٰتِهِنَّ وَلَا نِسَآئِهِنَّ وَلَا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُهُنَّ ۗ وَٱتَّقِينَ ٱللَّهَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدًا (٥٥)
There is no sin on them [in socializing freely] with their fathers, or their sons, or their brothers, or their brothers’ sons, or the sons of their sisters, or their own womenfolk, or what their right hands own. Be wary of Allah. Indeed Allah is witness to all things. (55)
إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ وَمَلَـٰٓئِكَتَهُۥ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى ٱلنَّبِىِّ ۚ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ صَلُّوا۟ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا۟ تَسْلِيمًا (٥٦)
Indeed Allah and His angels bless the Prophet; O you who have faith! Invoke blessings on him and invoke Peace upon him in a worthy manner. (56)
إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ لَعَنَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا مُّهِينًا (٥٧)
Indeed those who offend Allah and His Apostle are cursed by Allah in the world and the Hereafter, and He has prepared a humiliating punishment for them. (57)
وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ بِغَيْرِ مَا ٱكْتَسَبُوا۟ فَقَدِ ٱحْتَمَلُوا۟ بُهْتَـٰنًا وَإِثْمًا مُّبِينًا (٥٨)
Those who offend faithful men and women undeservedly, certainly bear the guilt of slander and flagrant sin. (58)
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ قُل لِّأَزْوَٰجِكَ وَبَنَاتِكَ وَنِسَآءِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يُدْنِينَ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِن جَلَـٰبِيبِهِنَّ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ أَدْنَىٰٓ أَن يُعْرَفْنَ فَلَا يُؤْذَيْنَ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (٥٩)
O Prophet! Tell your wives and your daughters and the women of the faithful to draw closely over themselves their chadors [when going out]. That makes it likely for them to be recognized and not be troubled, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (59)
۞ لَّئِن لَّمْ يَنتَهِ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ وَٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ وَٱلْمُرْجِفُونَ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ لَنُغْرِيَنَّكَ بِهِمْ ثُمَّ لَا يُجَاوِرُونَكَ فِيهَآ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (٦٠)
If the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness, and the rumourmongers in the city do not desist, We will prompt you [to take action] against them; then they will not be your neighbours in it except briefly. (60)
مَّلْعُونِينَ ۖ أَيْنَمَا ثُقِفُوٓا۟ أُخِذُوا۟ وَقُتِّلُوا۟ تَقْتِيلًا (٦١)
Accursed, they will be seized wherever they are confronted and slain violently: (61)
سُنَّةَ ٱللَّهِ فِى ٱلَّذِينَ خَلَوْا۟ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّةِ ٱللَّهِ تَبْدِيلًا (٦٢)
Allah’s precedent with those who passed away before, and you will never find any change in Allah’s precedent. (62)

(Commentary)

The verses contain various rulings, some of which are specific to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and his wives, while others are general.

His saying, the Exalted: "O you who have faith! When you marry faithful women and then divorce them before you touch them, there shall be no period for them that you must reckon. So provide for them and release them in a graceful manner" (33:49). What is meant by "marrying them" (نكاحهن) is the concluding of the marriage contract, and by "touching" (المس) is sexual intercourse (الدخول). "Providing for them" (التمتيع) means giving them an amount of wealth suitable to their status and circumstances, and "releasing them in a graceful manner" (التسريح بالجميل) means letting them go without dispute or harshness.

The meaning is: If you divorce women after the marriage contract but before intercourse, they are not required to observe a waiting period (العدة) — the prescribed period a woman must wait after divorce or the death of her husband before she can remarry — for that divorce, and it is obligatory to provide for them with some wealth and a graceful release.

The verse is absolute (مطلقة), covering both cases where a dower (المهر) has been specified and where it has not. It is thus restricted (يقيدها) by His saying: "And if you divorce them before you have touched them, and you have already settled a dowry for them, then [pay them] half of what you have settled" (2:237). Thus, the current verse remains the authoritative proof (حجة) for cases where no dower was specified.

His saying, the Exalted: "O Prophet! Indeed We have made lawful for you your wives whom you have given their dowries" (33:50) until the end of the verse. The Glorified mentions seven categories of women made lawful for His Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). The first category is in His saying: "your wives whom you have given their dowries"; what is meant by "rewards" (الأجور) here is the dowers (المهور). The second is in His saying: "and those whom your right hand owns from what Allah has given you as spoils of war" (ما ملكت يمينك مما أفاء الله عليك), meaning the female slaves he owns who came to him from the spoils (الغنائم) and the public wealth (الأنفال). The qualification of "what your right hand owns" as being "from what Allah has given you as spoils" is like the qualification of the wives by His saying "whom you have given their dowries"—it is for clarification (للتوضيح), not for exclusion (للاحتراز).

The third and fourth categories are in His saying: "and the daughters of your paternal uncle and the daughters of your paternal aunts." It is said this refers to the women of Quraysh. The fifth and sixth are in His saying: "and the daughters of your maternal uncle and the daughters of your maternal aunts." It is said this refers to the women of Banu Zuhrah. Regarding His saying: "who emigrated with you," it is stated in al-Majma‘: "This was only the case before the lawfulness of non-emigrant women; then the condition of emigration for lawfulness was abrogated (نسخ)."

The seventh category is in His saying: "and a faithful woman if she offers herself to the Prophet and the Prophet desires to take her in marriage." This refers to a Muslim woman who offers herself to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), meaning she consents to marry him without a gift or dower (صداق ومهر); Allah made her lawful for him if he wished to marry her. His saying: "specially for you, exclusive of the [rest of the] faithful" is an announcement that this ruling—the lawfulness of a woman to a man through her offering herself—is among his specific privileges (خصائصه) and does not apply to the rest of the believers. His subsequent saying: "We certainly know what We have made obligatory for them concerning their wives and those whom their right hands own" is an affirmation of this ruling of exclusivity.

His saying: "so that there may be no blame on you" is a justification (تعليل) for His saying at the beginning of the verse: "Indeed We have made lawful for you," or for the ruling of exclusivity at its end; the former is more apparent. The verse concludes with forgiveness and mercy.

His saying, the Exalted: "You may put off whomever you wish of them, and take to yourself whomever you wish" (33:51) etc. "Putting off" (الإرجاء) means delaying and distancing, which is an allusion (كناية) to rejection. "Taking to oneself" (الإيواء) means housing in a place, which is an allusion to acceptance and joining to oneself.

The context indicates that the meaning is that he (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) has the choice to accept or reject those who offer themselves to him.

His saying: "and as for any you may seek of those you had set aside, there is no sin upon you." "Seeking" (الابتغاء) is to request; meaning, those you seek from among those you had set aside and did not initially accept, there is no sin or blame upon you. That is, it is permissible for you to join to yourself those you had set aside and rejected from the women who offered themselves to you after the initial setting aside and rejection.

It is also possible that this is an indication that he (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) has the right to distribute his time among his wives or to waive the distribution (القسم). Thus, he may delay whomever he wishes and prioritize whomever he wishes, and set aside some from the distribution so he does not allot time to her, or seek her out and allot time to her after having set her aside. This is more in line with His subsequent saying: "and as for any you may seek of those you had set aside, there is no sin upon you. That is more likely to delight their eyes"—meaning they will be happy—"and they will not grieve, and all of them will be pleased with what you give them. Allah knows what is in your hearts." This is because the prioritized wife is happy with the time allotted to her, and the delayed wife hopes to be prioritized later.

His saying: "And Allah is All-knowing, All-clement" means He knows the best interests of His servants and does not hasten to punishment.

There are other various opinions regarding this verse, but what we have mentioned is most consistent with its occurrence in the context of the preceding verse and its connection to it, and this is what has been narrated from the Imams of the Ahl al-Bayt (عليه‌السلام), as will follow.

His saying, the Exalted: "Beyond that, women are not lawful for you, nor that you should replace them with other wives, even though their beauty should attract you" (33:52) etc. The apparent meaning of the verse, if assumed to be independent and not connected to what precedes it, is the prohibition of women for him (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) except for those who were given the choice and chose Allah; the denial of the permissibility of replacing them supports this.

However, if it is assumed to be connected to what precedes it—namely His saying: "Indeed We have made lawful for you" etc.—then its meaning is the prohibition of anyone other than those enumerated, which are the six categories mentioned previously.

In some narrations from some of the Imams of the Ahl al-Bayt (عليه‌السلام), it is stated that what is meant by the verse is the prohibited categories of women enumerated in His saying: "Forbidden to you are your mothers, your daughters..." (4:23).

Thus, His saying: "Beyond that, women are not lawful for you" means: after those who chose Allah and His Messenger, who were nine, according to the first meaning; or after those We enumerated in Our saying: "Indeed We have made lawful for you," according to the second meaning; or after the lawful ones, meaning the prohibited categories, according to the third meaning.

His saying: "nor that you should replace them with other wives" means to divorce some of them and marry others in their place. His saying: "except those whom your right hand owns" refers to female slaves, and it is an exception from His saying at the beginning of the verse: "women are not lawful for you."

His saying: "And Allah is watchful over all things" has an apparent meaning and contains a warning against opposition.

His saying, the Exalted: "O you who have faith! Do not enter the Prophet’s houses unless permission is granted you"—until His saying—"from the truth" (33:53). This is an explanation of the etiquette (أدب) of entering the houses of the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). His saying: "unless permission is granted you" is an exception from the prohibition. His saying: "for a meal" is connected to the permission. His saying: "without waiting for it to be readied" (غَيْرَ ناظِرِينَ إِناهُ) means without waiting for the food to be prepared by entering early and staying a long time in anticipation of the meal. This is clarified by His saying: "But when you are invited, enter, and when you have eaten, disperse." His saying: "without lingering for the sake of chat" is coordinated with His saying: "without waiting for it to be readied," describing one state after another; meaning, do not stay while waiting for the food before the meal, nor while lingering for chat after the meal.

His saying: "That used to hurt the Prophet, but he would be shy of [asking] you [to leave]" is the justification for the prohibition. That is, do not stay like that because your staying used to cause the Prophet distress, yet he would be shy to ask you to leave. His saying: "and Allah is not shy of the truth" means He is not shy of clarifying the truth to you, which is mentioning his distress and teaching the appropriate etiquette.

His saying, the Exalted: "And when you ask them for any object, ask them from behind a curtain. That is purer for your hearts and their hearts" (33:53). The pronoun in "ask them" (سَأَلْتُمُوهُنَّ) refers to the wives of the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). Asking them for an object is an allusion to speaking to them for a need; meaning, if a need arises for you to speak to the wives of the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), then speak to them from behind a curtain (حجاب). His saying: "That is purer for your hearts and their hearts" is an explanation of the benefit of the ruling.

His saying, the Exalted: "You should not hurt the Apostle of Allah, nor should you ever marry his wives after him" (33:53) etc. Meaning, it is not for you to hurt him by opposing what you have been commanded regarding his wives or otherwise, nor are you ever to marry his wives after him. Indeed, that—your marrying his wives after him—is a grave matter with Allah. In the verse, there is an intimation that some of them mentioned something suggesting they would marry his wives after him, and that was indeed the case, as will come in the following discussion of narrations.

His saying, the Exalted: "Whether you disclose anything or hide it, Allah indeed has knowledge of all things" (33:54). Its meaning is apparent, and it is in reality a threatening reminder to whoever was hurting the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) or mentioning the marriage of his wives after him.

His saying, the Exalted: "There is no sin on them [if they appear] before their fathers" (33:55) until the end of the verse. The pronoun in "on them" (عَلَيْهِنَّ) refers to the wives of the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). The verse is in the sense of an exception from the general ruling of the veil (الحجاب). It has excepted fathers, sons, brothers, sons of brothers, and sons of sisters; these are the unmarriageable kin (المحارم). It is said that paternal and maternal uncles were not mentioned because they might describe the wives to their own sons.

It also excepted "their women" (نساءهن). The annexation of "women" to the pronoun "them" suggests that what is meant is the believing women, excluding the disbelieving ones, as previously mentioned regarding His saying: "or their women" (24:31). It also excepted those whom their right hands own, including both male and female slaves.

His saying: "and be God-conscious of Allah. Indeed Allah is witness to all things" contains an emphasis of the ruling, especially through the shift (التفات) from the third person to the second person in "be God-conscious of Allah" (وَاتَّقِينَ اللهَ).

His saying, the Exalted: "Indeed Allah and His angels bless the Prophet. O you who have faith! Invoke blessings on him and invoke Peace upon him in worthy fashion" (33:56). It has been mentioned previously that the root of salat (الصلاة) is "turning toward" (الانعطاف). Thus, His blessing, the Exalted, is His turning toward him with mercy—an absolute turning not restricted in the verse to one thing over another. Likewise, the blessing of the angels upon him is their turning toward him with sanctification and seeking forgiveness, and from the believers, it is the prayer for mercy.

In mentioning His blessing, the Exalted, and the blessing of His angels upon him before commanding the believers to bless him, there is an indication that the believers' blessing for him is an emulation of Allah, the Glorified, and His angels, and an emphasis of the subsequent prohibition.

Narrations have reached the level of being widely transmitted (استفاضت) through both Shi'i and Sunni chains that the way for the believers to bless him is to ask Allah, the Exalted, to bless him and his Progeny (آله).

His saying, the Exalted: "Indeed those who offend Allah and His Apostle are cursed by Allah in the world and the Hereafter, and He has prepared for them a humiliating punishment" (33:57). It is well known that Allah, the Glorified, is transcendent above being reached by offense or anything carrying the stain of deficiency or lowliness. Thus, mentioning Him with the Messenger and partnering Him in the offense against him is an honoring of the Messenger and an indication that whoever targets His Messenger with evil has also targeted Him with evil, for the Messenger, qua Messenger, has none but his Lord; so whoever targets him has targeted his Lord.

He has promised them the curse (اللعن) in this world and the Hereafter. The curse (اللعن) — the removal and distancing from the mercy of Allah — is the removal from mercy. The specific mercy for the believers is guidance to the true belief and the reality of faith (الإيمان), followed by righteous deeds (العمل الصالح). Thus, removal from mercy in this world is its prohibition upon them as a recompense for their deeds, which results in the sealing of hearts, as He said: "We cursed them and made their hearts hard" (5:13), and He said: "but Allah has cursed them for their unfaith, so they do not believe, except a few" (4:46), and He said: "These are they whom Allah has cursed, so He made them deaf and blinded their sight" (47:23).

As for the curse in the Hereafter, it is the removal from the mercy of proximity therein, as the Exalted said: "No indeed! They will be barred from their Lord on that day" (83:15).

Then He promised them that He has prepared for them—meaning in the Hereafter—a humiliating punishment. The punishment is described as "humiliating" (مهينا) because through their arrogance in this world, they intended to humiliate Allah and His Messenger, so they are met in the Hereafter with a punishment that humiliates them.

His saying, the Exalted: "And those who offend faithful men and faithful women without their having committed any [fault], they certainly bear the guilt of calumny and a flagrant sin" (33:58). The qualification of their offense as being "without their having committed any [fault]" is because offending them for what they have committed—as in retaliation (القصاص), prescribed punishments (الحد), and discretionary punishments (التعزير)—carries no sin.

As for offending them without cause and without desert, Allah considers it a bearing of calumny (البهتان) and flagrant sin. Calumny (البهتان) — the act of falsely accusing someone to their face — is lying about another to their face. The reason why an offense without cause is considered calumny is that the offender only offends due to a reason they consider a crime, saying: "Why did he say such-and-such?" or "Why did he do such-and-such?" when it is not a crime; thus, they calumniate them during the offense by attributing a crime to them to their face when it is not a crime.

It is a "flagrant sin" (إثماً مبيناً) because the intellect perceives slander and calumny to be sins without the need for a religious prohibition to be revealed.

His saying, the Exalted: "O Prophet! Tell your wives and your daughters and the women of the faithful to draw their cloaks over themselves" (33:59) etc. "Cloaks" (جلابيب) is the plural of jilbab (جلباب), which is a garment a woman wraps herself in so that it covers her entire body, or the head-covering (الخمار) with which she covers her head and face.

His saying: "to draw their cloaks over themselves" (يُدْنِينَ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ جَلَابِيبِهِنَّ) means they should cover themselves with them so that their necklines and chests are not visible to onlookers.

His saying: "That is more likely to distinguish them so that they are not harrassed" means that covering the entire body is closer to them being recognized as people of modesty and righteousness, so they are not harassed; that is, the profligate will not harass them by accosting them.

It is also said the meaning is: that is closer to them being recognized as free Muslim women, so they are not accosted under the assumption that they are slaves or non-Muslim women from the People of the Book or others; the first meaning is more likely.

His saying, the Exalted: "If the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness and the rumor-mongers in Madinah do not desist, We will surely rouse you against them" (33:60) etc. "Desisting" (الانتهاء) from something is to refrain and cease from it. "Rumor-mongering" (الإرجاف) is the spreading of falsehood to cause grief and provoke agitation. "Rousing" (الإغراء) to an action is to incite toward it.

The meaning is: I swear, if the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness do not cease from corruption, and those who spread false news in Madinah to provoke agitation among the Muslims, We will surely incite you against them; then they will not be your neighbors in Madinah due to their expulsion from it, except for a little while—which is the time between the issuance of the command and its actual execution.

His saying, the Exalted: "Accursed, they will be seized wherever they are confronted and slain a [ruthless] slaying" (33:61). "Confronting" (الثقف) is to come upon something and overtake it. The phrase is a circumstantial qualifier (حال) for the hypocrites and those coordinated with them; meaning, in the state of being accursed, wherever they are found, they shall be seized and their killing shall be intensified until it encompasses them all.

His saying, the Exalted: "Allah’s precedent with those who passed away before, and you will never find any change in Allah’s precedent" (33:62). A "precedent" (السنة) is the established way that proceeds by its nature usually or always.

The Glorified says: This exemplary punishment with which We have threatened the hypocrites and those who follow their path—of expulsion and ruthless killing—is the precedent of Allah that proceeded among those of the past. Whenever a people went to extremes in corruption and provoking agitation among the people, and persisted and transgressed in that, We seized them likewise. You will never find any change in Allah’s precedent, so it shall proceed among you just as it proceeded among the nations before you.

( Discussion of Narrations )

In al-Faqih, ‘Amr ibn Shimr narrated from Jabir, from Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: "and then divorce them before you touch them, there shall be no period for them that you must reckon. So provide for them and release them in a graceful manner"; he said: "Provide for them (متعوهن), meaning give them gifts of what you are able from recognized kindness (معروف), for they return with sadness, loneliness, great anxiety, and the mockery of their enemies. Indeed, Allah is Generous, He is shy and loves the people of shyness; the most honorable of you is the one who is most honorable to his consorts."

In al-Kafi, with its chain to al-Halabi from Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) regarding a man who divorces his wife before he has entered upon her. He said: "He owes half the dower if he had specified something for her; and if he had not specified for her, then he must provide for her (فليمتعها) in the manner that women like her are provided for."

I say: The narrations with this meaning are numerous, and they are based on the specification (تخصيص) of this verse by the verse of al-Baqarah, as mentioned in the commentary of the verse.

In al-Durr al-Manthur, ‘Abd ibn Humayd extracted from Habib ibn Thabit who said: A man came to ‘Ali ibn al-Husayn and asked him about a man who said: "If I marry so-and-so, she is divorced." He said: "It is nothing; Allah began with marriage before divorce, for He said: 'O you who have faith! When you marry faithful women and then divorce them...'"

I say: It was also narrated in al-Majma‘ from Habib ibn Thabit from him (peace be upon him).

In the same source, Ibn Majah and Ibn Mardawayh extracted from al-Miswar ibn Makhramah from the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) who said: لا طلاق قبل نكاح ولا عتق قبل ملك — "There is no divorce before marriage, and no manumission before ownership."

I say: The like of it was narrated from Jabir and ‘Aishah from him (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله).

In al-Kafi, with its chain to al-Hadrami from Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) and with its chain to al-Halabi from Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: "O Prophet! Indeed We have made lawful for you your wives"; how many women were made lawful for him? He said: "Whatever he wished."

In the same source, with its chain to al-Halabi from Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him) who said: I said: "Beyond that, women are not lawful for you, nor that you should replace them with other wives"? He said: "It was for the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) to marry whomever he wished of the daughters of his paternal uncle and the daughters of his paternal aunts, and the daughters of his maternal uncle and the daughters of his maternal aunts, and his wives who emigrated with him. And it was made lawful for him to marry from the generality of the believers without a dower, which is the gift (الهبة); and the gift is not lawful except for the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). As for anyone other than the Messenger of Allah, no marriage is valid except with a dower. That is the meaning of His saying, the Exalted: 'and a faithful woman if she offers herself to the Prophet'."

In al-Durr al-Manthur, Ibn Sa‘d, Ibn Abi Shaybah, ‘Abd ibn Humayd, Ibn Jarir, Ibn al-Mundhir, and al-Tabarani extracted from ‘Ali ibn al-Husayn regarding His saying: "and a faithful woman"; she is Umm Sharik al-Azdiyyah who offered herself to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله).

I say: It is also narrated that she was Khawlah bint al-Hakim, and that she was Layla bint al-Khatim, and that she was Maymunah. It appears that those who offered themselves were a number of women.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Muhammad ibn Qays from Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) who said: A woman from the Ansar came to the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and said: "O Messenger of Allah, a woman does not propose to a husband, and I am a widow with no husband for a long time, nor a child; do you have any need? If so, I have offered myself to you if you accept me." The Messenger of Allah spoke well to her and prayed for her. Then he said: "O sister of the Ansar, may Allah reward you well on behalf of the Messenger of Allah; your men supported me and your women desired me." Hafsah said to her: "How little is your shyness, how bold you are, and how intense is your desire for men!" The Messenger of Allah said: "Desist from her, O Hafsah, for she is better than you; she desired the Messenger of Allah while you blamed and faulted her." Then he said to the woman: "Depart, may Allah have mercy on you; Allah has made Paradise obligatory for you because of your desire for me and your seeking my love and pleasure. My command will reach you, if Allah wills." Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: "and a faithful woman if she offers herself to the Prophet and the Prophet desires to take her in marriage, specially for you, exclusive of the [rest of the] faithful." He said: "So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made lawful the woman's offering of herself to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), and that is not lawful for anyone else."

In al-Majma‘: It is said that when she offered herself to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), ‘Aishah said: "What is the matter with women that they give themselves without a dower?" Then the verse was revealed, and ‘Aishah said: "I see that Allah only hastens to fulfill your desire." The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: "Indeed, if you obey Allah, He will hasten to fulfill your desire."

In al-Majma‘ regarding His saying, the Exalted: "You may put off whomever you wish of them, and take to yourself whomever you wish"; Abu Ja‘far and Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon them) said: "Whoever he put off, he did not marry, and whoever he took in, he married."

In al-Kafi, with its chain to al-Hadrami from Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: "Beyond that, women are not lawful for you"; he said: "He only meant by it: the women whom Allah forbade to you in this verse: 'Forbidden to you are your mothers, your daughters, your sisters, your paternal aunts, your maternal aunts...' until its end. If the matter were as they say, He would have made lawful for you what He did not make lawful for him, because any of you can replace [wives] whenever he wishes. But the matter is not as they say; indeed Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made lawful for His Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) to marry of women whatever he wished, except what He forbade in this verse in Surah al-Nisa’."

In al-Durr al-Manthur, ‘Abd ibn Humayd, Ibn al-Mundhir, and Ibn Abi Hatim extracted through the path of ‘Ali ibn Zayd from al-Hasan regarding His saying: "nor that you should replace them with other wives"; he said: "Allah restricted him to his nine wives with whom he died." ‘Ali said: "So I informed ‘Ali ibn al-Husayn, and he said: 'Had he wished, he could have married others.'" And in the wording of ‘Abd ibn Humayd: "Rather, it was also for him to marry others."

In the Tafsir of al-Qummi: Regarding His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: "O you who have faith! Do not enter the Prophet’s houses unless permission is granted you"; when the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) married Zaynab bint Jahsh—and he loved her—he gave a wedding feast and invited his companions. When his companions ate, they liked to converse in the presence of the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), while he wished to be alone with Zaynab. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: "O you who have faith! Do not enter the Prophet’s houses unless permission is granted you"; that was because they used to enter without permission. So the Mighty and Majestic said: "unless permission is granted you" until His saying "from behind a curtain."

I say: The details of the story are narrated from Anas through various paths.

In al-Durr al-Manthur, Ibn Sa‘d extracted from Salih ibn Kaysan who said: "The veil (حجاب) of the Messenger of Allah was revealed over his wives in Dhu al-Qa‘dah in the fifth year of the Hijrah."

I say: It was also narrated by Ibn Sa‘d from Anas, and in it: "That year was the consummation of the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) with Zaynab."

In the same source regarding His saying, the Exalted: "You should not hurt..." the verse: Ibn Abi Hatim extracted from al-Suddi who said: "It reached us that Talhah ibn ‘Ubaydillah said: 'Does Muhammad veil us from the daughters of our paternal uncles and then marry our women after us? If something happens to him, we shall surely marry his wives after him.' So the verse was revealed."

I say: Several narrations have been reported to this effect, and in some of them, it is mentioned that he intended ‘Aishah and Umm Salamah.

In Thawab al-A‘mal, from Abu al-Mu‘adh from Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) in a hadith, he said: I said: "What is the meaning of the blessing of Allah, the blessing of His angels, and the blessing of the believer?" He said: "The blessing of Allah is mercy from Allah, the blessing of the angels is sanctification (تزكية) from them for him, and the blessing of the believers is a prayer (دعاء) from them for him."

In al-Khisal, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in the Hadith of the Four Hundred, he said: صلوا على محمد وآل محمد ـ فإن الله تعالى يقبل دعاءكم عند ذكر محمد — "Invoke blessings upon Muhammad and the Progeny of Muhammad, for Allah, the Exalted, accepts your prayer upon the mention of Muhammad; and [He accepts] your prayer and your preservation of him when you recite: 'Indeed Allah and His angels bless the Prophet'; so bless him whether you are in prayer or otherwise."

In al-Durr al-Manthur, ‘Abd al-Razzaq, Ibn Abi Shaybah, Ahmad, ‘Abd ibn Humayd, al-Bukhari, al-Muslim, Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa’i, Ibn Majah, and Ibn Mardawayh extracted from Ka‘b ibn ‘Ujrah who said: A man said: "O Messenger of Allah, as for the peace (السلام) upon you, we have known it; but how is the blessing (الصلاة) upon you?" He said: "Say: O Allah, bless Muhammad and the Progeny of Muhammad, as You blessed Ibrahim and the Progeny of Ibrahim; indeed You are All-laudable, All-glorious. O Allah, prosper Muhammad and the Progeny of Muhammad, as You prospered Ibrahim and the Progeny of Ibrahim; indeed You are All-laudable, All-glorious."

I say: He cited eighteen ahadith other than this narration indicating the inclusion of the Progeny of the Prophet with him in the blessing, narrated by the authors of the Sunan and Jawami‘ from several companions, including Ibn ‘Abbas, Talhah, Abu Sa‘id al-Khudri, Abu Hurayrah, Abu Mas‘ud al-Ansari, Buraydah, Ibn Mas‘ud, Ka‘b ibn ‘Ujrah, and ‘Ali (peace be upon him). As for the Shi'i narrations, they are beyond counting.

In the same source, Ahmad and al-Tirmidhi extracted from al-Hasan ibn ‘Ali that the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: البخيل من ذكرت عنده فلم يصل علي — "The miser is he in whose presence I am mentioned and he does not invoke blessings upon me."

In the Tafsir of al-Qummi regarding His saying, the Exalted: "O Prophet! Tell your wives and your daughters and the women of the faithful to draw their cloaks over themselves"; the occasion of its revelation was that women used to go out to the mosque and pray behind the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). When it was night and they went out for the Maghrib and ‘Isha’ prayers, young men would sit in their path and harass them and accost them. So Allah revealed: "O Prophet!" the verse.

In al-Durr al-Manthur, ‘Abd al-Razzaq, ‘Abd ibn Humayd, Abu Dawud, Ibn al-Mundhir, Ibn Abi Hatim, and Ibn Mardawayh extracted from Umm Salamah who said: "When this verse was revealed: 'to draw their cloaks over themselves', the women of the Ansar went out as if there were crows upon their heads from the black garments they wore."

In the Tafsir of al-Qummi regarding His saying, the Exalted: "If the hypocrites... do not desist"; it was revealed concerning a group of hypocrites who were in Madinah, spreading rumors (يرجفون) about the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) when he went out on some of his expeditions, saying: "He has been killed and captured," causing the Muslims to grieve. They complained to the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), so Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: "If... do not desist" until His saying "except for a little while"; meaning, We shall command you to expel them from Madinah except for a little while.

"Accursed, they will be seized wherever they are confronted and slain a [ruthless] slaying." In the narration of Abu al-Jarud from Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him), he said: "Accursed (ملعونين); thus the curse became mandatory upon them, curse after curse, by the word of Allah."

Ayahs

يَسْـَٔلُكَ ٱلنَّاسُ عَنِ ٱلسَّاعَةِ ۖ قُلْ إِنَّمَا عِلْمُهَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَا يُدْرِيكَ لَعَلَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ تَكُونُ قَرِيبًا (٦٣)
The people question you concerning the Hour. Say, ‘Its knowledge is only with Allah.’ What do you know, maybe the Hour is near. (63)
إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَعَنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ سَعِيرًا (٦٤)
Indeed Allah has cursed the faithless and prepared for them a blaze, (64)
خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۖ لَّا يَجِدُونَ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا (٦٥)
in which they will remain forever, and will not find any guardian or helper. (65)
يَوْمَ تُقَلَّبُ وُجُوهُهُمْ فِى ٱلنَّارِ يَقُولُونَ يَـٰلَيْتَنَآ أَطَعْنَا ٱللَّهَ وَأَطَعْنَا ٱلرَّسُولَا۠ (٦٦)
The day when their faces are turned about in the Fire, they will say, ‘We wish we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Apostle!’ (66)
وَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ إِنَّآ أَطَعْنَا سَادَتَنَا وَكُبَرَآءَنَا فَأَضَلُّونَا ٱلسَّبِيلَا۠ (٦٧)
They will say, ‘Our Lord! We obeyed our leaders and elders, and they led us astray from the way.’ (67)
رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِهِمْ ضِعْفَيْنِ مِنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ وَٱلْعَنْهُمْ لَعْنًا كَبِيرًا (٦٨)
Our Lord! Give them a double punishment and curse them with a mighty curse.’ (68)
